vocabulary.definition: The sum of all values divided by the number of values (average)
The middle value when data is ordered from least to greatest
The value that appears most often in a data set
A data value that is much greater or less than most other values
The shape and spread of a data set
vocabulary.visual: Symmetric, skewed left, or skewed right

vocabulary.definition: How spread out the values in a data set are

launch.narrative: You're the stats analyst for the school basketball team. Coach needs a report on the team's scoring: in the last 7 games they scored 42, 38, 55, 41, 38, 44, and 40 points. Which average best represents the team's typical performance?
launch.contextImage: Basketball scoreboard with stat sheets and a clipboard
noticePrompts: What are the highest and lowest scores?
Which score seems like a typical game for this team?
wonderPrompts: Will the mean and median be the same number?
How does the 55-point game affect the average?

explore.label: Order the scores on the number line, then find the mean, median, and mode. Place markers at: A = Mean, B = Median, C = Mode.

discourse.prompt: The mean (42.6 ≈ 42) is higher than the median (41). Why? Which better represents a 'typical' game?
discourse.sentenceFrame: The mean is higher than the median because the outlier score of ___ pulls the mean ___. The ___ better represents a typical game because ___.

onLevel.label: Match each term to its definition.

extending.title: Find the Stats Error
extending.correctWork: Mode = 15 (appears twice, more than any other value). Mode is the most frequent value, not the largest.
hints: Mode means 'most often,' not 'most.' Which number appears the most times?
15 appears twice. No other number repeats. So the mode is 15, not 18.

extending.prompt: Two students measured their height every month for 5 months. Student A's MAD is 0.5 inches. Student B's MAD is 2.3 inches. What does this tell you?
extending.sentenceFrame: Student A's measurements are more ___ because ___. Student B's measurements ___ because ___.

connect.scenario: Your class took a math quiz. The scores were: 85, 92, 78, 88, 92, 95, 72, 88, 92, 84. The teacher says 'most students scored around 87.' A student disagrees and says 'the most common score was 92.'
connect.promptQuestion: Who is using the mean, and who is using the mode? Which is more useful here?
connect.prompt: The teacher used the ___ (about 86.6). The student used the ___ (92). The ___ is more useful here because ___.

exitTicket.stem: Data set: 4, 7, 10, 7, 12. What is the median?
